eligible_applicant_type: Privately owned commercial or mixed-use property owners
Graduates of the Community-Based Real Estate Development Training program (2020-2023)
project_area: Community-driven revitalization of mixed-use commercial districts, including stabilization measures, facade renovations, interior building upgrades, and site improvements for commercial or mixed-use buildings
demographic_requirements: Strong preference for projects involving at-risk historic buildings, projects in identified Commercial District target areas, and projects initiated by graduates of the Community-Based Real Estate Development Training program (2020-2023)
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants must own a commercial or mixed-use property located on Buffalo’s East Side. Projects must involve stabilization, facade, interior, or site improvements. Applicants must contribute 10% of the total project cost (50% for Anchors initiative, with 10% as equity). Small Projects awards range from $100,000 to $400,000, while Anchors awards range from $400,000 to $2,000,000. Additional eligibility criteria include project location in target areas, historic preservation, and training program participation.
